1200003156.english: And you said it, didn't you?

1200003157.english: Orca is not yours, you are Orca's. Hmph.
1200003157.korean: 오르카가 너의 것이 아니라 네가 오르카의 것이라고. 후후.

1200003158.english: Now I'm part of Orca too.

1200003159.english: Now you're mine too, aren't you?

1200003160.english: I look forward to working with you.

1200003162.english: - I know how much Fafnir loves treasure. I know you're in love.
1200003162.korean: - 나는 파프니르가 보물을 얼마나 좋아하는지. 사랑하는 지 알고 있다.

1200003163.english: - Because of this, I could guess what emotions were behind Fafnir calling me a treasure.
1200003163.korean: - 그러했기에 파프니르가 나를 보물이라고 칭하는 것에 무슨 감정이 담겨있는 지 짐작할 수 있었다.

1200003164.english: - Feeling my heart warm, I decided to tell Fafnir something as well.
1200003164.korean: - 나는 가슴이 따뜻해지는 것을 느끼며 나 역시도 파프니르에게 한 가지 알려주기로 했다.

1200003168.english: - Long or short. Thick or thin. Whether it's a lot or a little. Whether it has fur or not, whether it's made of metal or not.
1200003168.korean: - 길든, 짧든. 굵든, 가늘든. 많든, 적든. 털이 났든, 털이 나지 않았든, 기계로 되었든.

1200003169.english: - All of Orca's tails are mine.

1200003170.english: - I've had my eye on Fafnir's tail since the first time I saw it.
1200003170.korean: - 파프니르의 꼬리를 처음 봤을 때부터 마음에 두고 있었다.

1200003174.english: - The contact area decreased, but the visual stimulation increased.
1200003174.korean: - 닿는 면적은 줄었지만 시각적인 자극은 늘었다.

1200003176.english: - If my front tail doesn't react in this situation, humanity will be destroyed.
1200003176.korean: - 지금 이 상황에서 내 앞꼬리가 반응하지 않으면 인류는 멸망하겠지.

1200003177.english: - Luckily, it seems humanity won't be destroyed.

1200003178.english: - Judging by the reaction of my front tail.

1200003179.english: - I spent precious time with my new precious person, who protected my precious people.
1200003179.korean: - 나는 나의 소중한 사람들을 지켜준, 나의 새로운 소중한 사람과 함께 소중한 시간을 보냈다.

1200003181.english: Still, I should be more cautious when dealing with Iron Blooded Beasts.
1200003181.korean: 그래도 철충을 상대할 때 더 신중해야겠어.

1200003183.english: Are you planning some kind of festival?

1200003186.english: Okay. Give me the luggage. I'll listen.

1200003187.english: Fortune. Did you get the information about the haka from Sterling?
1200003187.korean: 포츈. 스털링한테서 하카에 대한 자료 받았지?

1200003188.english: Then let's make one more haka.

1200003189.english: It would be difficult to be exactly the same. Reduce the size.

1200003190.english: Haka was really useful this time, wasn't it? It will be just as useful even if you reduce the size.
1200003190.korean: 이번에 하카가 엄청 유용했잖아. 크기를 줄여도 똑같이 유용할 거야.

1200003194.english: Sneaking into men's rooms isn't your hobby, is it?
1200003194.korean: 남자 방에 몰래 숨어드는 게 취미는 아니지?

1200003195.english: So, can I assume you consider me a treasure?
1200003195.korean: 그러면 나를 보물로 여긴다고 생각해도 되겠네?

1200003197.english: Can I ask just one thing?

1200003198.english: Why did you come to Orca?

1200003199.english: Did you think it was possible?

1200003202.english: Would you like me to do it?

1200003203.english: I don't think Fafnir is a fool.
1200003203.korean: 나는 파프니르를 바보라고 생각 안 해.

1200003207.english: Then you should also know about Fafnir.

1200003208.english: As if I belong to all the Orcas.

1200003209.english: All of Orca's tails are mine.

1200003210.english: Escort the survivors to a safe zone and informed Orca that residents had been kidnapped.
1200003210.korean: 생존자를 안전지대로 호위하고 오르카에 주민들이 납치되었다고 알렸다.

1200003212.english: Wouldn't it be better to wait for reinforcements to arrive?
1200003212.korean: 지원군이 오기를 기다리는 것이 낫지 않겠습니까?

1200003213.english: Since we do not know what the Iron Bug's purpose is, it is right to rescue the residents as quickly as possible.
1200003213.korean: 철충들의 목적이 뭔지 알 수 없는 이상 최대한 빨리 주민들을 구하는 것이 옳다.

1200003214.english: Wouldn't the chances of success be higher if we went with reinforcements rather than forcing ourselves to rescue them?
1200003214.korean: 무리해서 구하기보다는 지원군과 함께 하는 쪽이 더 성공확률이 높지 않겠습니까?

1200003215.english: Veronica, are you still reluctant to save the residents?
1200003215.korean: 베로니카, 너는 여전히 주민들을 구하는 것이 내키지 않나?

1200003216.english: The residents are important, but I do not want the angels to sacrifice themselves in vain.
1200003216.korean: 주민들도 중요하지만 저는 헛되이 천사님들께서 희생하는 것을 바라지 않습니다.

1200003217.english: Shouldn't we save the angels just as much as we save the residents?
1200003217.korean: 주민들을 구해야 하는 것만큼이나 천사님들도 구해야 하지 않겠습니까.

1200003218.english: We are certain that even if we die, we will return to the embrace of the light.
1200003218.korean: 우리는 죽더라도 빛의 품으로 돌아갈 거라는 확신이 있어요.

1200003219.english: But that isn't the case for the residents who used to live here. For them, death is the end.
1200003219.korean: 하지만 이곳에 살던 주민들은 아니죠. 그녀들에게 죽음은 끝이에요.

1200003220.english: We cannot let the poor souls face death in anxiety and fear.
1200003220.korean: 불쌍한 자들을 불안과 공포 속에 죽음을 맞이하게 둘 수는 없어요.

1200003222.english: I think it is right for me to manage the denomination's budget.
1200003222.korean: 역시 교단의 예산은 제가 관리하는 것이 맞는 거 같습니다.

1200003224.english: Because the angels will be able to lead the believers to the right path without any hesitation.
1200003224.korean: 천사님들께서 아무런 고민 없이 신도들을 올바른 길로 이끌 수 있을 테니까요.

1200003226.english: Following the teachings of the angels…

1200003227.english: I will go to save the poor.

1200003228.english: Fortunately, the Iron Bugs don't hide their tracks.

1200003232.english: So the Iron Bug were experimenting on the Bio-roids.

1200003234.english: Or perhaps we just didn't know that it was something we were already doing?
1200003234.korean: 아니면 원래 하던 것을 우리가 모르고 있었던 걸까요?

1200003238.english: The traces here… are all recent.
1200003238.korean: 여기 있는 흔적들… 전부 최근의 것들입니다.

1200003239.english: I don't know if the Iron Bug is cleaning hard...
1200003239.korean: 철충이 청소를 열심히 하는 것이라면 모르겠지만…

1200003240.english: I can't imagine an Iron Bug cleaning.
1200003240.korean: 철충이 청소를 하는 모습을 떠올릴 수가 없군요.

1200003241.english: And although I am not an expert on machinery, the equipment here appears to have been made relatively recently.
1200003241.korean: 그리고 제가 기계에 해박한 것은 아니지만 여기 있는 장비들 비교적 최근에 만들어진 것 같습니다.

1200003242.english: There are no signs of discoloration or repairs.

1200003243.english: You can worry about it later.

1200003244.english: The traces here are not even that recent.
1200003244.korean: 여기 있는 흔적은 너무 최근의 것도 아니다.

1200003245.english: It means there is a high probability that the residents are still safe.
1200003245.korean: 주민들이 아직 무사할 확률이 높다는 의미지.

1200003246.english: If they weren't conducting an experiment right now, they would have locked the residents up somewhere.
1200003246.korean: 지금 실험을 진행하고 있는 것이 아니라면 주민들을 어디에 가뒀을 것이다.

1200003247.english: Now is not the time to lament and worry.

1200003248.english: The Light said firmly, "Do not hesitate in the slightest when doing good. If you think it is right, go straight like the sword of light."
1200003248.korean: 빛께서 단호히 가로되. 선을 행할 때에는 조금의 주저도 하지 말지어다. 네가 옳다 생각한다면 빛의 검처럼 곧게 나아가라.

1200003249.english: Let's rescue the survivors, even if it means destroying the entire research facility.
1200003249.korean: 이 연구소를 모두 부수는 한이 있더라도 생존자들을 구출하자.

1200003251.english: We rescued everyone, but my legs are slow!

1200003252.english: I will bind the feet of the Ironclad! Go first, angels!
1200003252.korean: 제가 철충의 발을 묶겠습니다! 먼저 가십시오, 천사님들!

1200003253.english: Veronica! You said it! We need to save ourselves just as much as we need to save the residents!
1200003253.korean: 베로니카! 당신이 말했잖아요! 주민들을 구해야 하는 것만큼이나 저희들도 구해야 한다고요!

1200003255.english: Don't try to carry it all alone!

1200003256.english: Will the Savior's sword break easily?

1200003259.english: I will escort the survivors with my drones.

1200003260.english: So, please rest assured and engage in battle.

1200003265.english: No... don't try to be funny right now.

1200003266.english: Okay. Everyone, step back from the firing line.

1200003268.english: If we stand in a straight line, we can finish early.
1200003268.korean: 일직선으로 서 있으면 일찍 끝낼 수 있지.

1200003269.english: ...Huh? Did you just unconsciously make a joke?

1200003273.english: The survivors are all safe too!

1200003274.english: They've all been loaded onto the vehicles for evacuation.

1200003275.english: Then we'll escort the cars until the very end and return.

1200003276.english: Thank you for your help, Strikers!

1200003277.english: I just did what I was supposed to do.
1200003277.korean: 당연히 해야 할 일을 했는 걸요.

1200003279.english: And as someone who can help others.
1200003279.korean: 그리고 남을 도울 수 있는 자로서.

1200003281.english: I can finally breathe a sigh of relief.

1200003283.english: Saving one person is like saving the world.
1200003283.korean: 한 사람을 구함은 곧 세상을 구함이다.

1200003285.english: As you may already know, this is a quote from the Talmud.
1200003285.korean: 이미 알고 계시겠지만 탈무드의 한 구절입니다.

1200003286.english: While people may interpret this passage differently...
1200003286.korean: 이 구절이 어떤 의미인지 사람들마다 해석이 달라지겠지만…

1200003287.english: For this moment today, I will interpret it this way.
1200003287.korean: 저는 오늘 이 순간만큼은 이렇게 해석하겠습니다.

1200003288.english: By saving people... I saved my faith and belief.
1200003288.korean: 사람들을 구함으로서… 저의 믿음과 신앙을 구했다고요.

1200003289.english: If I hadn't saved them, the mark would have remained on me for the rest of my life.
1200003289.korean: 저들을 구하지 못했다면 저의 남은 생에 계속 흔적이 남았겠죠.

1200003290.english: Please continue to guide me on the right path, Azazel.
1200003290.korean: 앞으로도 저를 올바른 길로 이끌어주십시오, 아자젤 님.

1200003292.english: Of course, the road to get here is quite rough, but...
1200003292.korean: 물론 여기까지 오는 길이 많이 험하기는 한데…

1200003293.english: There's already more than enough time for a round trip.

1200003294.english: Did something happen on the way?

1200003295.english: Or maybe an accident on the way?

1200003296.english: Or maybe something happened to Orca?

1200003298.english: Is it right to wait? Or is it right to move forward, even if it means forcing myself?
1200003298.korean: 기다리는 게 옳을까? 아니면 무리해서라도 움직이는 것이 옳을까?

1200003299.english: Haha… You really make it so I don't have to worry. Thanks.
1200003299.korean: 하하… 고민할 필요 없게 만들어주네. 고맙게.

1200003300.english: I'll smash you guys to pieces as a token of gratitude.

1200003301.english: Come what may! I'm taking everyone and leaving this place!
1200003301.korean: 죽이 되든 밥이 되든! 모두를 데리고 여기를 떠나겠어!

1200003303.english: We'll arrive at the next village just a little further on!
1200003303.korean: 조금만 더 가면 다음 마을에 도착하니까!

1200003304.english: I'll be able to rest there!

1200003305.english: (I couldn't even properly pack food or water because I fled in such a hurry.)
1200003305.korean: (급하게 도망친다고 식량도 물도 제대로 못 챙겼네.)

1200003308.english: Ah… I’m so, so, so grateful that it makes it so I don’t have to worry!
1200003308.korean: 아… 정말 정말 정말 고맙게도 고민할 필요가 없게 만들어주네!

1200003311.english: I am the Ace of Clover, the symbol of happiness and friendship!
1200003311.korean: 나는 행복과 우정의 상징 클로버 에이스!

1200003312.english: I will punish anyone who tries to shatter another's happiness!
1200003312.korean: 남의 행복을 깨트리려는 자는 내가 응징하겠어!

1200003314.english: Gasp! That's a lot of you guys! Gasp! Isn't that too many?
1200003314.korean: 허억! 너희들 숫자가! 허억! 너무 많은 거 아냐?

1200003315.english: You guys got pretty crushed too. How about we both back off?
1200003315.korean: 너희도 많이 박살났는데. 서로 물러나는 건 어때?

1200003317.english: Damn it! Fine! Let's fight to the death!

1200003318.english: It’s not a good day to die, miss!

1200003321.english: Now that I've shown up, I won't let you guys run wild as you please!
1200003321.korean: 내가 등장한 이상 너희들이 마음대로 설치게 두지 않겠어!

1200003326.english: The crime of invading and stealing the Bio-Roids' homeland!

1200003327.english: The crime of forcibly taking over and modifying the bodies of the AGS!
1200003327.korean: AGS들의 몸을 강제로 빼앗고 개조한 죄!

1200003328.english: The sin of destroying and developing nature!

1200003329.english: The crime of destroying cultural heritage!

1200003330.english: The crime of using and destroying public property!

1200003331.english: The crime of using weapons illegally!

1200003332.english: The crime of destroying the economy and order!

1200003335.english: The crime of attacking my friend!

1200003336.english: According to the authority granted to me!

1200003341.english: Because it was the sheriff's job, of course.

1200003343.english: The dialog when he appeared was terrible.

1200003345.english: But the entrance itself was cool.

1200003347.english: Well... I was always cool anyway.

1200003349.english: Can you get to the destination?

1200003350.english: Of course! I'm the Ace of Clubs, the symbol of happiness and friendship!
1200003350.korean: 당연하지! 나는 행복과 우정의 상징 클로버 에이스니까!

1200003351.english: I want to say that, but I'm a little tired.

1200003352.english: It doesn't seem like just a little bit.

1200003355.english: Then, can I trouble you for a little help?

1200003359.english: Didn't I say I'd hit you when you got back?

1200003360.english: Are you still holding onto that?

1200003361.english: Again, a sheriff needs a good memory.
1200003361.korean: 다시 말하지만 보안관은 기억력이 좋아야 하니까.

1200003362.english: That way, it's easier to catch wanted criminals.

1200003363.english: The part about having to deal with villains is the same.

1200003366.english: I saved you, and you're late with your thanks.

1200003368.english: The fact that you called me a friend.

1200003370.english: It's not something a symbol of friendship and happiness should say, but...
1200003370.korean: 우정과 행복의 상징이 할 말은 아니지만…

1200003371.english: Aren't you a little embarrassed to say you're friends so confidently?
1200003371.korean: 당당하게 친구라고 말하면 조금 부끄럽지 않아?

1200003374.english: What pirates do is plunder and steal. So, it's only natural that the commander would fall for me... but it took him so long to confess! Do you know how much I was looking forward to this? Hmph, I'll let it slide this time because you're the person I love. From now on, do I need you to tell me you love me every day so I don't get angry?
1200003374.korean: 해적이 하는 일은 약탈하고 빼앗는 거지. 그러니까 사령관이 나한테 마음을 빼앗기는 것도 당연한 일이야… 그치만 고백하기까지 너무 오래 걸렸어! 내가 얼마나 기대하고 있었는지 알아? 흥, 내가 사랑하는 사람이니까 한 번 봐준다. 앞으로는 나 화 안 나게 매일매일 사랑한다고 해줘야 해?

1200003387.english: Second in command, Io. You trust me, right?

1200003388.english: ...This is definitely the first time I'm hearing this, but why does it sound so familiar?
1200003388.korean: …이거 분명 처음 듣는 말인데, 어째 되게 익숙하게 들린다?

1200003389.english: ...Um, Spriggan... is this, by any chance, an extension of the conversation you had with Captain Nashorn and Callista the other day?
1200003389.korean: …저기, 스프리건… 이거 혹시, 저번에 나스호른 대장님이랑 칼리스타한테 했던 얘기의 연장선이야?

1200003391.english: I was told to be careful around Calista...

1200003392.english: Oh, you don't mean that... Life in Orca or whatever it's called, do you?
1200003392.korean: 아, 설마 그… 라이프 인 오르카인가 뭔가 하는 거?

1200003393.english: Didn't that get caught by the publicity department last time and banned from broadcasting?
1200003393.korean: 그거 저번에 홍보부에 걸려서 방송 금지 당하지 않았냐?

1200003394.english: ...Ahaha, that's how it was before. I was a bit... lacking in delicacy.
1200003394.korean: …아하하, 전에는 그랬지. 내가 좀… 델리케이트한 면이 부족했어.

1200003396.english: Thanks to the mental training I received from Alpha-nim, I was reborn as a true journalist and successfully created a video with absolutely no problems!
1200003396.korean: 알파님께 받은 정신교육 덕분에 참된 기자로 다시 태어난 나는, 정말 아무런 문제가 없는 영상을 만드는 것에 성공했다고!

1200003398.english: ...Of course, depending on your perspective, there are some parts that could be considered slightly problematic.
1200003398.korean: …물론 관점에 따라서 살짝 문제삼을 수 있는 부분이 있기는 하지.

1200003399.english: ...I hate myself for trusting you, even for a moment.

1200003400.english: ...But generally speaking, it's a video that's not a problem! I'm telling you, it's true!
1200003400.korean: …하지만 일반적으로 봤을 때는 문제 없는 영상이야! 정말이라니까?

1200003402.english: Of course! Of course. As evidence to support my words, isn't the commander here with us too?
1200003402.korean: 그러엄! 그렇고 말고. 내 말을 뒷받침하는 증거로, 사령관도 여기 같이 와 있잖아?

1200003404.english: I don't know why I can't trust you.
1200003404.korean: 왜 이렇게 믿음이 안 가는지 모르겠네.

1200003405.english: Ahahaha, let's talk for a bit, just the two of us...
1200003405.korean: 아하하하, 잠시 둘이서 얘기 좀 할게…

1200003406.english: What are you doing, Commander! Didn't we agree to stick to the same story when we talked earlier?
1200003406.korean: 뭐 하는 거야, 사령관! 분명히 아까 얘기했을 때는 말 맞춰 주기로 했잖아!

1200003407.english: - But that was before I knew the other person was Panther.
1200003407.korean: - 하지만 그건 상대가 팬서라는 것을 몰랐을 때의 일이다.

1200003408.english: - If I'm up against Panther, who always works so hard, I can't help but feel guilty...
1200003408.korean: - 항상 고생하는 팬서가 상대라면 나도 양심의 가책을 느낄 수밖에 없다…

1200003409.english: - Beside, Panther was already in debt in various ways recently... because of the Nashorn.
1200003409.korean: - 게다가 최근 팬서에게는… 나스호른 때문에도 이래저래 빚을 지고 있는 상태였으니까.

1200003410.english: ...Okay. It's true that the deputy commander is having a hard time.

1200003411.english: So, all the more... shouldn't the commander lighten the burden on the deputy commander?
1200003411.korean: 그러니까 더더욱… 사령관이 부대장의 일거리를 덜어줘야 하지 않겠어?

1200003412.english: - Saying that, Spriggan shook the data stick in front of my eyes...
1200003412.korean: - 그렇게 말하며 스프리건은 내 눈 앞에서 데이터 스틱을 흔들었다…

1200003413.english: Hehe... It's not a big deal, just the record of the Iron-Blooded activity in the area we're in charge of this time. I thot the commander might enjoy having a good time by reviewing it once, so I quietly grabbed a copy.
1200003413.korean: 헤헷… 대단한 건 아니고, 이번에 우리가 담당한 구역의 철충 활동 기록이야. 사령관이 한번쯤 검토하면서 좋은 시간을 보낼 수 있지 않을까 해서 슬쩍 복사본을 챙겨뒀지.

1200003414.english: Of course, Panther's deputy commander will write the report because he's diligent. But...
1200003414.korean: 물론 팬서 부대장은 성실하니까 보고서는 쓸 거야. 하지만…

1200003415.english: Wouldn't it be a good thing if the commander also took a look once in a while to understand the trends of the Iron Blood, so that the time Panther Squad Leader spends briefing in detail would be reduced?
1200003415.korean: 사령관도 한 번쯤 보면서 철충의 동향을 파악하면 팬서 부대장이 상세하게 브리핑을 하는 시간이 줄어들 테니 좋은 일이잖아?

1200003416.english: Of course... that means the commander will have more work to do.
1200003416.korean: 물론… 그만큼 사령관에게는 일거리가 생기겠지만 말이야.

1200003417.english: - Spriggan... to offer me a condition I can't possibly refuse...!
1200003417.korean: - 스프리건… 내가 절대 거절할 수 없는 조건을 제시하다니…!

1200003418.english: If the commander cooperates... this data could just slip right into the commander's pocket... What do you think?
1200003418.korean: 사령관이 협조만 해 준다면… 이 자료가 사령관의 주머니 속으로 쏙 하고 들어갈 수도 있는데 말이지… 어떻게 생각해?

1200003419.english: Bribe? Haha, what are you talking about, Commander?
1200003419.korean: 뇌물? 하하, 무슨 말을 하는 거야, 사령관?

1200003420.english: If I gave the commander a can of tuna while asking him a favor, that would be a bribe, wouldn't it? But...
1200003420.korean: 내가 사령관에게 부탁을 하면서 참치캔을 건넸다면 그건 뇌물이겠지. 하지만…

1200003421.english: ...Is it really bribery to receive a job?
1200003421.korean: …과연 일거리를 받는 것을 뇌물이라고 할 수 있을까?

1200003422.english: Okay. This is absolutely not a bribe, Commander...
1200003422.korean: 그래. 이건 절대 뇌물이 아니란 말이야, 사령관…

1200003423.english: No one can call it a bribe when someone offers you a job. Even Orca's laws can't punish this...!"
1200003423.korean: 그 누구도 일거리를 던져주는 걸 뇌물이라고 할 순 없단 말이지. 심지어 오르카의 법으로도 이 일을 처벌할 수는 없어…!

1200003424.english: So, now you know what you need to do, right?
1200003424.korean: 그럼 이제 뭘 해야 할지 알겠지?

1200003425.english: All you have to do is testify in front of those two people. There's nothing wrong with my video!
1200003425.korean: 저 두 사람 앞에서 증언하기만 하면 돼. 내 영상에는 아무런 문제가 없다고…!

1200003426.english: - The moment I took the data stick with trembling hands...
1200003426.korean: - 내가 덜덜 떨리는 손으로 데이터 스틱을 받아드는 순간…

1200003427.english: Do you think that will work?

1200003428.english: - Bang! With a shout, the Bloody Panther's fist slammed into the Spriggan's head.
1200003428.korean: - 쾅! 하는 소리와 함께 블러디 팬서의 주먹이 스프리건의 머리를 강타했다.

1200003429.english: Wow... wow... it sounded like a huge noise...
1200003429.korean: 와… 와아… 엄청난 소리가 난 것 같은데…

1200003434.english: Ugh... it hurts so much I can't even scream...
1200003434.korean: 으윽… 너무 아파서 소리도 못 지르겠어…

1200003435.english: It's a set to seduce the naive commander.

1200003437.english: Second-in-command, no matter how much the commander likes you, isn't your judgment a bit clouded?
1200003437.korean: 부대장, 아무리 사령관이 좋아도 그렇지… 콩깍지가 너무 많이 씌워져 있는 거 아니야?

1200003438.english: ...Do you want another hit? Or do you want me to zip your mouth shut?
1200003438.korean: …한대 더 맞을래? 아니면 입에 지퍼 채울래?

1200003439.english: By the way, this time I'm going to hit you for real, like when I hit the captain's back.
1200003439.korean: 참고로 이번에는 대장 등짝 때릴 때처럼 진심으로 때린다.

1200003441.english: Okay. Go back quietly and prepare for training.
1200003441.korean: 오냐. 얌전히 돌아가서 훈련 준비나 해라.

1200003442.english: ...But, before you zip it up, just one word... just let me say one word...!
1200003442.korean: …하지만, 지퍼 채우기 전에 한 마디만… 한 마디만 하게 해줘…!

1200003445.english: Could you please take a look at it first and then let me know what you think?
1200003445.korean: 일단 한 번 보고 생각해주면 안 될까?

1200003446.english: This time it's a real, proper video, so you can look forward to it...
1200003446.korean: 이번에는 진짜 제대로 된 영상이니까, 기대해도 좋아…

1200003447.english: It covers how the Orca crew members are living now! They're videos that show their true selves.
1200003447.korean: 지금 오르카 대원들이 어떻게 살고 있는지 다룬! 진솔한 모습을 나타내는 영상들이라고.

1200003448.english: Just trust me this once, okay?

1200003452.english: ...Still, they must have worked hard on the video, so wouldn't it be okay to watch it once...?
1200003452.korean: …그래도 영상은 열심히 만들었을 텐데, 한 번 봐 주는 것 정도는 괜찮지 않을까…?

1200003453.english: Sigh... If even the commander says so... alright.

1200003454.korean: 좋아, 그럼 어디 한 번 보여 줘 봐.

1200003455.english: Alright! As expected, he's the vice-commander!

1200003458.english: No, this is because you said you'd treat... Ugh!
1200003458.korean: 아니, 이건 네가 한턱 낸다고 해서… 읍!

1200003459.english: That's how it was back then...

1200003462.english: Even if you try to tempt me with bribes like this, it won't work...!

1200003465.english: Okay. Even after they said that much...

1200003469.english: Yes. Now that I've registered the beta with the internal security system, we should be able to minimize security procedures. And, one more thing...
1200003469.korean: 네. 이걸로 내부 보안 시스템에 베타를 등록했으니, 보안 절차를 최소화할 수 있을 거예요. 그리고, 하나 더…

1200003470.english: I sent the network key. You should now be able to access the Orca intranet.
1200003470.korean: 네트워크 키를 전송했어요. 이제 오르카 인트라넷에 접속할 수 있을 거예요.

1200003471.english: Of course, most networks will be transitioning to the open internet rather than an intranet, but...
1200003471.korean: 물론 대부분의 네트워크는 인트라넷이 아니라 개방형 인터넷으로 전환할 예정이지만…

1200003472.english: Still, since Orca is a military organization, they can't completely eliminate the intranet.
1200003472.korean: 그래도 오르카는 엄연히 군사집단이니까 인트라넷을 완전히 없앨 수는 없거든요.

1200003473.english: Please be careful not to leak or get your network key hacked!
1200003473.korean: 네트워크 키가 유출되거나 해킹당하지 않게 조심해 주세요!

1200003474.english: Thank you... I'll keep it safe.

1200003475.english: Well, if it's a beta, there shouldn't be any problems. Because he's an expert at hacking.
1200003475.korean: 뭐, 베타라면 문제 없겠죠. 해킹의 전문가니까요.

1200003476.english: ...to the point where they manipulated their physical data as soon as they joined Orca.

1200003477.english: City, what do you mean by manipulating physical data? I, I have no idea about this at all!!
1200003477.korean: 시, 신체 데이터 조작이라뇨? 저, 저는 전혀 모르는 일인데요!!

1200003478.english: Really? But when I weighed myself in Caracas, my weight was definitely...
1200003478.korean: 그래요? 하지만 카라카스에서 쟀을 때 체중이 분명…

1200003480.english: ...And didn't you, Alpha, manipulate your physical data as soon as you joined?
1200003480.korean: …그러는 알파님도 합류하자마자 신체 데이터를 조작하시지 않았나요?

1200003482.english: ...Ahem, I was going to say that sisters tend to resemble each other.
1200003482.korean: …크흠, 자매는 닮는다는 얘기를 하려고 했던 거예요.

1200003484.english: Oh, anyway, since there's nothing left to do today, go and get some rest. You have a health checkup scheduled tonight, right?
1200003484.korean: 아, 아무튼, 오늘은 더 이상 남은 일이 없으니 가서 푹 쉬세요. 이따가 밤에 건강검진 일정도 있잖아요?

1200003486.english: Then... I'll be heading in now. Alpha, Skadi, have a good day.
1200003486.korean: 그러면… 저는 이만 들어가 볼게요. 알파 언니, 스카디 님, 좋은 하루 되세요.

1200003487.english: Have a good day too, Beta.

1200003489.english: (Ugh, I'm so tired... I can't even drink water because I have a blood test, I just want the health checkup to be over quickly...)
1200003489.korean: (으아, 피곤해… 피검사 해야 하니까 물도 못 마시고, 빨리 건강검진이 끝났으면 좋겠다…)

1200003490.english: (I should set an alarm and try to get some sleep...)

1200003492.english: (I heard all sorts of video materials are uploaded to the Orca Secret Network... So... that...)
1200003492.korean: (오르카 시크릿 네트워크에는 각종 영상 자료가 업로드 되어 있다던데… 그러면… 그…)

1200003493.english: (Master, um... do you... have videos like that too...?)

1200003495.english: (Oh, no...! What am I thinking right now...!)

1200003496.english: (I, I should stop doing strange things and just go to sleep...)
1200003496.korean: (이, 이상한 짓 하지 말고 잠이나 자야겠어요…)

1200003498.english: (...But wouldn't it be okay to check it out briefly anyway?)
1200003498.korean: (…그래도 잠깐 확인해 보는 건 괜찮지 않을까요?)

1200003499.english: (Yes, it will be okay. Above all, this is an action to serve the master even better...!)
1200003499.korean: (그래요, 괜찮을 거예요. 무엇보다 이건 주인님께 더 잘 봉사하기 위한 행동이니까요…!)

1200003500.english: (If you connect with the network key...)

1200003502.english: ...Even so, it's embarrassing that the search history remains... I need to connect to the server and delete the logs...
1200003502.korean: …아무리 그래도 검색 기록이 남는 건 부끄러우니까… 서버에 접속해서 로그를 지워야…

1200003503.english: ...Huh, what's this post that has so many comments?
1200003503.korean: …어라, 이 글은 뭐길래 이렇게 댓글이 많이 달린 걸까요?

1200003504.english: The title is... let me see...

1200003505.korean: …<매지컬 젠틀맨과 뜨거운 밤 보냄, 사진 첨부>…?

1200003507.english: This, this kind of post could be inappropriate and violate public morals, so I'll have to check it myself...
1200003507.korean: 이, 이런 글은 미풍양속에 위반될 만한 부적절한 글일 수도 있으니까, 제가 직접 확인을 해 봐야겠어요…

1200003508.english: Look! This is the masterpiece that Magical Gentleman and I stayed up all night to create together!
1200003508.korean: 봐라! 이것이 매지컬 젠틀맨과 내가 함께 밤을 새서 만들어낸 걸작이다!

1200003509.english: It's a firework that explodes in the shape of the Great Demon King Faucre's face!
1200003509.korean: 바로 뽀끄루 대마왕님의 얼굴 모양으로 폭발하는 폭죽이지!

1200003511.english: Wow, that's amazing. Are you planning to set it off at the festival later?
1200003511.korean: 와, 멋지네요. 나중에 축제에서 터뜨리실 생각인가요?

1200003512.english: It will be very beautiful, like a flower falling in splendor.
1200003512.korean: 화려하게 지는 꽃처럼 아주 아름다울 것 같아요.

1200003513.english: That's ridiculous! I can't blow up something that captures the charismatic appearance of the Great Demon King!
1200003513.korean: 말도 안 되는 소리! 대마왕님의 카리스마 넘치는 모습을 담고 있는 물건을 터뜨릴 수는 없다!

1200003514.english: This will be preserved forever as a treasure of our Demon King's army!!
1200003514.korean: 이건 우리 마왕군의 보물로 영원히 보존할 것이다!!

1200003515.english: ...If that's the case, why was it made out of fireworks? Fireworks are meant to be set off to fulfill their purpose.
1200003515.korean: …그럴 거면 왜 폭죽으로 만든 건가? 폭죽은 터뜨려야 본래의 소임을 다하는 것인데.

1200003516.english: When Ms. Azaz made indoor fireworks with the leftover gunpowder, I thot it was a chance to show off the Demon King's magnificent appearance, so I just helped her make them.
1200003516.korean: 아자즈 양이 남는 화약으로 실내용 폭죽을 만들길래, 마왕님의 멋진 모습을 알릴 기회라고 생각해 옆에서 함께 만든 것 뿐이다.

1200003519.english: It's like words that should never be together are stuck together.
1200003519.korean: 절대 같이 있으면 안 되는 단어끼리 붙은 것 같은데.

1200003520.english: Well, isn't that something that happens all the time in Orca?
1200003520.korean: 뭐, 그런 거야 오르카에서 흔하게 벌어지고 있는 일 아닌가?

1200003523.english: Hey, you gloomy person, I'm sleeping in your room tonight.
1200003523.korean: 야 음침이, 나 오늘 네 방에서 잔다.

1200003525.english: Draculina, if you wanted to have a pajama party, you should have told me beforehand...
1200003525.korean: 드라큐리나, 파자마 파티를 하고 싶던 거라면 미리 말씀해 주시지…

1200003526.english: For Draculina's pajamas, should I prepare the ones she wore the last time the master and we spent the night together?
1200003526.korean: 드라큐리나의 파자마는 저번에 주인님과 저희가 함께 밤을 보낼 때 입었던 걸로 준비하면 될까요?

1200003527.english: Hey!! I told you to throw away those perverted cat pajamas!!!
1200003527.korean: 야!! 내가 그 변태 같은 고양이 파자마는 버리라고 했잖아!!!

1200003528.english: But when Draculina wore it, she seemed more assertive than usual...
1200003528.korean: 하지만 드라큐리나가 그걸 입으니까 평소보다 더 적극적이던 것 같아서…

1200003529.english: ...Don't say things like that on a public forum!!

1200003530.english: And it's not that we're having a pajama party!!
1200003530.korean: 그리고 파자마 파티를 한다는 게 아니라!!

1200003531.english: Given Azaz's personality, if he made something like that, wouldn't he blow it up in the name of experimentation?
1200003531.korean: 아자즈 성격 상 그런 걸 만들면 실험한답시고 터뜨려 볼 거 아니야.

1200003533.english: And chances are, I'll set it off in my room.
1200003533.korean: 그리고 높은 확률로 내 방에서 터뜨릴 테고.

1200003534.english: So, since my room would have been a mess anyway, you're saying you'd rather sleep in your room than see it in that state.
1200003534.korean: 그럼 어차피 내 방은 엉망이 되었을 테니까, 그 꼴을 보느니 차라리 네 방에서 잔다는 얘기라고.

1200003535.english: Oh, no, that's not the case. There's no way Azaz would do something so inconsiderate, right?
1200003535.korean: 어머, 그렇지 않아요. 아자즈가 그렇게 몰상식한 행동을 할 리가 없잖아요?

1200003536.english: ...It's okay for me to be angry, right?

1200003537.english: The Frost Serpent is here!! Whenever something happens, they always check my room first! Because you guys keep causing trouble in my room!!
1200003537.korean: 프로스트 서펀트가!! 무슨 일이 생기면 일단 내 방을 체크한단 말이야!! 니들이 하도 내 방에서 이것저것 사고를 쳐서!!

1200003538.english: But what about the lack of common sense and all that?!

1200003539.english: But... those indoor fireworks were made to show to Draculina.
1200003539.korean: 하지만… 그 실내용 폭죽은 드라큐리나에게 보여주기 위해 만든 거라서요.

1200003540.english: You wouldn't set off fireworks when Dracula isn't around, would you?
1200003540.korean: 드라큐리나가 없을 때 폭죽을 터뜨릴 리가 없잖아요?

1200003541.english: Oh, that's what you were talking about. Of course you would.

1200003542.english: So, actually, I was on my way to find Draculina with Azaz right now.
1200003542.korean: 그래서 사실 지금 아자즈와 함께 드라큐리나를 찾으러 가고 있었어요.

1200003544.english: Please wait a moment, I'll show you right away...

1200003545.english: ... No, wait a minute! Was this the incident you mentioned last time?

1200003546.english: It's okay if you don't show it! Don't come! Don't come!
1200003546.korean: 안 보여줘도 괜찮아! 오지 마! 오지 말라고!

1200003547.english: You heard me, right? I told you not to come!!

1200003548.english: It seems like the connection has already been terminated.

1200003550.english: If I may offer you a piece of advice, Miss Draculina...
1200003550.korean: 내 충고 하나 하자면 말일세, 드라큐리나 낭자…

1200003551.english: You'd better run away as quickly as possible.
1200003551.korean: 최대한 빨리 도망치는 게 좋을 걸세.

1200003553.english: Well, I'm not sure about that, but...

1200003554.english: Please try not to run to where I am. You know, these things are always most fun when you're just watching from afar.
1200003554.korean: 되도록 내가 있는 곳으로 도망치지는 말아주게. 원래 이런 건 강 건너 불구경일 때가 가장 재밌거든.

1200003555.english: ...Where are you! Just know that I'm going to run away to where you are, no matter what!
1200003555.korean: …너 어딨어! 무조건 너 있는 곳으로 도망칠 거니까 그런 줄 알아!

1200003557.english: ...Then, I've just remembered an urgent matter... I'll take my leave now.
1200003557.korean: …그럼 소생은 급한 볼일이 생각나서… 이만 실례하도록 하지.

1200003560.english: Since you've come to my post, you should write a review about the fireworks that capture the magnificent appearance of the Demon King!
1200003560.korean: 기왕 내 글에 들어왔으면 마왕님의 멋진 모습을 담고 있는 폭죽에 대한 평가를 작성하고 가란 말이다!

1200003561.english: ...That was a completely different article than what I was expecting...

1200003562.english: Heh heh, still... it was nice to see how much fun the Orca people have in their daily lives.
1200003562.korean: 후후, 그래도… 오르카 분들이 평소 얼마나 즐겁게 생활하시는지 알 수 있어서 좋았어요.

1200003563.english: I should probably just read a few more interesting articles and then go to bed...
1200003563.korean: 재밌어 보이는 글 몇 개만 더 보고 자러 가야겠네요...

1200003564.english: The following text is... let's see...

1200003565.english: The greatest baseball team in history is, of course, the Pittsburgh Iron Maidens...?
1200003565.korean: <역사상 최고의 야구 팀은 역시 피츠버그 아이언 메이든>…?

1200003568.english: The greatest baseball team in history is, of course, the Pittsburgh Iron Maidens...
1200003568.korean: <역사상 최고의 야구 팀은 역시 피츠버그 아이언 메이든>…

1200003569.english: Who on earth wrote such a ridiculous article?
1200003569.korean: 대체 누가 이런 말도 안 되는 글을 쓴 거죠?

1200003570.english: The best baseball team is... the LA Cannoniers...

1200003572.english: Cerberus wrote something like this...? Something's not right...

1200003574.english: The best baseball team is the Pittsburgh Iron Maidens!

1200003575.english: That's right, that's right. Iron Maiden is the best, you know?

1200003576.english: Wow, look at them talking trash like it's before the apocalypse.
1200003576.korean: 와, 멸망 전 일이라고 막말하는 것 봐.

1200003577.english: Elvens is the best, you know? It was incredibly popular!

1200003578.english: ...Can't we just say both are great?
1200003578.korean: …둘 다 대단한 거로 하면 안 될까?

1200003579.english: No, I don't know about the commander, but this is a really important story!
1200003579.korean: 안돼, 사령관은 모르겠지만, 이건 정말 중요한 이야기라고!

1200003580.english: Elvens are definitely amazing, aren't they?

1200003581.english: The fact that I only got to hold the championship trophy once!
1200003581.korean: 우승컵을 한 번 밖에 못 들었다는 게!

1200003583.english: What can you do? It's true that Iron Maiden was a great band, tho.
1200003583.korean: 어쩌겠어? 아이언 메이든이 대단한 팀이었던 건 사실인데 뭐.

1200003584.english: It's a team formed by the meeting of Iron Annie, the ace pitcher of the Pittsburgh baseball team, and Armored Maiden, who boasted impenetrable defense! That's why it's called the Iron Maiden.
1200003584.korean: 피츠버그 야구팀의 에이스였던 아이언 애니와, 철벽 수비를 자랑하던 아머드 메이든이 만나서 만들어진 팀이잖아! 그래서 이름도 아이언 메이든인거고.

1200003585.english: It was a combination that was truly as perfect as the Commander and Iron Annie.
1200003585.korean: 그건 그야말로 사령관과 아이언 애니처럼 엄청나게 잘 어울리는 조합이었다고.

1200003588.english: I don't understand what you're saying.

1200003589.english: You're just taking sides because the same model did great, aren't you?
1200003589.korean: 같은 기종이 대활약했다고 해서 편 들어 주는 거잖아, 지금!

1200003590.english: What, what's that sound? I'm not an anime character, so I don't really know.
1200003590.korean: 무, 무슨 소리일까 그게… 난 애니가 아니라서 잘 모르겠는데?

1200003591.english: ...You chose that nickname and you're saying it's not an anime?

1200003592.english: You can come up with a nickname anytime, right? What if I'm actually Armand or the Clover Ace?
1200003592.korean: 닉네임이야 뭐 얼마든지 지을 수 있잖아? 내가 사실 아르망이나 클로버 에이스일 수도 있는 거라고.

1200003595.english: Prove that you're not Iron Annie.
1200003595.korean: 네가 아이언 애니가 아니라는 걸 증명해 보라고.

1200003596.english: I'm an Elven Forest Maker, and I'm currently at the Elven dormitory. If you come here and verify your Secret Network ID, I'll apologize for jumping to conclusions.
1200003596.korean: 나 엘븐 포레스트메이커인데 지금 엘븐 숙소에 있거든? 여기 찾아와서 시크릿 네트워크 아이디 인증하면 내가 넘겨짚은 거 사과한다.

1200003598.english: Oh... I just remembered something I need to do, so I'll be going now...!
1200003598.korean: 앗… 갑자기 할 일이 생각나서 난 이만…!

1200003601.english: ...Well, since we sent one of them, should we just let it go?

1200003602.english: Anyway, you should also get to know Cerberus and the Commander well...
1200003602.korean: 아무튼 켈베로스랑 사령관도 잘 알아 둬…

1200003603.english: The best baseball team is the Elvens!

1200003604.english: Hmph, I'll admit you defeated Annie. But...
1200003604.korean: 후후, 애니 씨를 물리친 건 인정해 드리죠. 하지만…

1200003605.english: Can you really refute my logic?
1200003605.korean: 과연 제 논리를 반박할 수 있을까요?

1200003607.english: You know, the reason the Pittsburgh Iron Maidens are the best baseball team.
1200003607.korean: 피츠버그 아이언 메이든이 최고의 야구팀인 이유 말이죠.

1200003608.korean: 하, 어디 한 번 들어나 보자.

1200003609.english: I'll admit that the dark elf from Elvens was an excellent pitcher.
1200003609.korean: 엘븐즈의 다크엘븐이 뛰어난 투수였다는 건 인정해요.

1200003610.english: But Iron Maiden's Io was just as good!
1200003610.korean: 하지만 아이언 메이든의 이오도 그에 못지 않았다고요!

1200003611.english: If the aces' skills are similar, wouldn't the Iron Maidens, who have the Bloody Panther, the outfielder with the best coverage in the league, be the better team?
1200003611.korean: 에이스들의 실력이 비슷하다면, 리그 최고의 커버범위를 가진 외야수 블러디 팬서가 있는 아이언 메이든이 더 나은 팀인 거 아니겠어요?

1200003612.english: Just a moment. That's a bit strange.

1200003614.english: ...I'm sorry, Master. But... I can't help but get involved in this story...!
1200003614.korean: …죄송합니다, 주인님. 하지만… 이 얘기에는 끼어들 수밖에 없어요…!

1200003615.english: Because the best baseball team isn't the Elvens or Iron Maiden!
1200003615.korean: 왜냐하면, 최고의 야구팀은 엘븐즈도, 아이언 메이든도 아니니까요!

1200003616.english: ...Oh, so where's the best place then?

1200003617.english: If you think about the essence of baseball, the answer comes quickly.
1200003617.korean: 야구의 본질을 생각하면 답은 금방 나오죠.

1200003618.english: Baseball is ultimately a game about scoring runs. Then wouldn't the LA Cannoniers, who had the league's best slugger, be the strongest team?
1200003618.korean: 야구는 결국 점수를 내는 게임. 그렇다면 리그 최고의 슬러거가 있던 LA 캐노니어즈가 가장 강한 팀 아니겠어요?

1200003621.english: That's quite unexpected, isn't it? Arsenal isn't very good at ball sports, are they?
1200003621.korean: 꽤나 의외인데? 아스널은 구기종목은 잘 못 하던데.

1200003622.english: Definitely, ball sports are my weakness.

1200003624.english: Because it seems like an interesting discussion is happening.
1200003624.korean: 재밌는 토론이 벌어지고 있는 것 같아서 말이지.

1200003625.english: Anyway, getting back to the main point... as you said, I'm not good at ball sports. But...
1200003625.korean: 아무튼, 다시 본론으로 돌아가서… 그대의 말대로, 난 구기종목에 약하다. 하지만…

1200003627.english: ...You're talking about a baseball bat, right?

1200003628.english: ...Captain, please be careful with statements that could be misunderstood. This bulletin board is for all ages, right?
1200003628.korean: …대장님, 오해를 살 수 있는 발언은 주의해 주세요. 이 게시판은 전체이용가라고요.

1200003630.english: And since Emily's class is about to end, please go and pick her up.
1200003630.korean: 그리고 에밀리 수업 끝날 시간이니까 가서 데려와 주세요.

1200003631.english: It's almost dinner time, so please don't buy any snacks on your way back.
1200003631.korean: 곧 저녁 먹을 시간이니까 올 때 간식 같은 거 사 주시면 안됩니다.

1200003634.english: Well, I'll be going now before I get scolded. Have a good time, my dear.
1200003634.korean: 그럼, 난 이만 혼나기 전에 가보도록 하지. 재밌는 시간 보내도록, 그대여.

1200003635.english: ...Now that I think about it, anonymous forums don't really mean much, do they?
1200003635.korean: …이제 와서 드는 생각인데 익명 게시판이라는 거 별로 의미 없지 않아?

1200003636.english: ...and the person whose nickname is "Commander" is saying that?
1200003636.korean: …닉네임을 사령관으로 지어 놓은 사람이 그런 말을 하는 거야?

1200003637.english: Anyway, the LA Cannoneers... yeah, I'll admit it. It's a good team with excellent balance between offense and defense.
1200003637.korean: 아무튼, LA 캐노니어즈… 그래, 인정할게. 공격과 수비의 밸런스가 뛰어난, 좋은 팀이지.

1200003638.english: But baseball isn't about numbers, it's about heart!
1200003638.korean: 하지만 야구는 숫자로 하는 게 아니야, 가슴으로 하는 거라고!

1200003639.english: Elvenz baseball has the power to stir people's emotions!
1200003639.korean: 엘븐즈의 야구에는 사람들의 감정을 자극하는 힘이 있어!

1200003640.english: Iron Maiden is the same, you know?

1200003641.english: The LA Cannoneers are also in a drama, aren't they? And...
1200003641.korean: LA 캐노니어즈도 한 드라마 하거든요? 그리고…

1200003642.english: Stop pretending to be a Cerberus, Sister Shepherd!
1200003642.korean: 켈베로스인 척을 하는 건 그만두시죠, 셰퍼드 언니!

1200003646.english: Since I came to Orca, I've been taking a lot of walks with Cerberus.
1200003646.korean: 전 오르카에 오고 나서 켈베로스와 함께 산책을 많이 했거든요.

1200003647.english: And during that walk, I promised Cerberus that if he spread the word that the best baseball team was the LA Cannoneers, he could have two walks a day from then on.
1200003647.korean: 그리고 그 산책시간 동안, 저는 켈베로스에게 최고의 야구팀은 LA 캐노니어즈라는 사실을 퍼뜨리고 다닌다면 앞으로는 하루 두 번씩 산책을 하게 해 준다고 약속했죠.

1200003649.english: So, Cerberus wouldn't write an article saying Iron Maiden is the best band!
1200003649.korean: 그러니 켈베로스가 아이언 메이든이 최고의 팀이라는 글을 쓸 리가 없어요!

1200003650.english: Damn, I can't believe I missed that point...

1200003651.english: ...I thot it was too ill-tempered for a Cerberus.

1200003652.english: ...Anyway, how did Sister Shepherd get in here?
1200003652.korean: …그나저나 셰퍼드 언니가 어떻게 여기 들어와 있는 거죠?

1200003653.english: Well, you see... the commander gave me the access code when I was lying in the hospital the other day.
1200003653.korean: 그야 뭐… 저번에 내가 병원에 누워 있을 때 사령관이 접속 코드를 주고 갔거든.

1200003654.english: You're talking about when you were defeated by Nidhogg, aren't you?

1200003655.english: Okay. And he said to contact him if I ever needed Orca's help.
1200003655.korean: 그래. 혹시나 오르카의 도움이 필요하면 연락하라고 하면서 말이지.

1200003656.english: So... what do you think, Commander? The best baseball team is definitely Iron Maiden, right?
1200003656.korean: 그래서… 어떻게 생각해, 사령관? 최고의 야구 팀은 역시 아이언 메이든이지?

1200003657.english: ...Why did the conversation get so heated in the first place? I just wrote a post asking to be taught about baseball...
1200003657.korean: …애초에 왜 이야기가 이렇게까지 과열된 거야? 난 그냥 야구에 대해서 알려달라고 글을 썼을 뿐인데…

1200003658.english: It's only natural to try and get a newbie to like your favorite team.
1200003658.korean: 뉴비한테 좋아하는 팀을 영업하는 건 당연한 거잖아.

1200003660.english: I've never even watched a baseball game!
1200003660.korean: 난 야구 경기도 본 적 없다고!

1200003661.english: Well, come to think of it, that makes sense...

1200003662.english: Sorry, Commander. I think I did something wrong.
1200003662.korean: 미안, 사령관. 내가 좀 잘못된 행동을 한 것 같아.

1200003663.english: ...I should have shown you Iron Maiden's fantastic baseball game first.
1200003663.korean: …일단 아이언 메이든의 환상적인 야구 경기부터 보여줬어야 했는데.

1200003664.english: No, that's not what I meant...

1200003666.english: Okay, let's look at this first and then talk.

1200003667.english: No, the loading still isn't finished. How big is the file size anyway...?
1200003667.korean: 아니, 로딩이 아직까지 안 끝나는데 용량이 대체 얼마나 큰 거야…?

1200003668.english: ...That's so mean, sis! If you're going to do that... I'll upload a video of the LA Cannoneers too!
1200003668.korean: …치사해요, 언니! 그렇게 나오신다면… 저도 LA 캐노니어즈 영상을 올릴 거예요!

1200003671.english: If you keep uploading such large files while the server is unstable like it is now...
1200003671.korean: 지금처럼 서버가 불안정한 상황에서 그런 고용량 파일을 마구 올려대면…

1200003672.english: They're so despicable! Does someone think there's no video?
1200003672.korean: 치사한 것들 같으니! 누군 영상 없는 줄 알아?

1200003673.english: Commander, do I have to watch the video I uploaded too? Got it?
1200003673.korean: 사령관, 내가 올린 영상도 꼭 봐야 해? 알겠지?

1200003675.english: Hey guys, I'll watch the video, but send that to my personal messages later.
1200003675.korean: 얘들아, 영상은 볼 테니까 그건 나중에 내 개인 메시지로-

1200003676.english: Fatal Error! Cannot connect to the server.
1200003676.korean: Fatal Error! 서버에 접속할 수 없습니다.

1200003678.english: I, I didn't expect this situation...
1200003678.korean: 이, 이런 상황은 예상 못 했는데…

1200003679.english: We have to escape quickly before the security team shows up...!
1200003679.korean: 보, 보안팀이 나타나기 전에 어서 도망쳐야…!

1200003681.english: Not only did they hack the server and delete the logs, but they even launched a DDoS attack...!
1200003681.korean: 서버를 해킹해서 로그를 지우는 걸로도 모자라서, 디도스 공격까지...!

1200003684.english: However, the strict management regulations of the towering Orca strictly prohibit network hacking! To hack in the first place and then expect mercy, how brazen!
1200003684.korean: 하지만 드높은 오르카의 관리 규정은 네트워크 해킹을 엄격히 금합니다! 애초에 해킹을 하고서 자비를 바라다니, 뻔뻔하시군요!

1200003685.english: Beta-nim is... banned from the Orca Network for 7 days!!

1200003687.english: It's my first offense, so please be lenient just this once...

1200003688.english: I was kicked out before I even had time to make an excuse...

1200003689.english: So, you want me to unblock you?

1200003690.english: I, I was wrong...! But, this will never happen again...
1200003690.korean: 제, 제가 잘못했어요…! 하지만, 다시는 이런 일 없을 거예요…

1200003691.english: I was obsessed with the LA Cannoneers until now, but I won't be anymore!
1200003691.korean: 그 동안은 LA 캐노니어즈에 집착했지만… 이제는 그러지 않을게요…!

1200003693.english: Beta, to be honest... I want to trust you. We're sisters, after all.
1200003693.korean: 베타, 솔직히 말하면… 당신을 믿고 싶어요. 저희는 자매니까요.

1200003694.english: If I were to quantify my trust in you... it would probably be 100%.
1200003694.korean: 당신을 향한 제 신뢰를 수치로 나타낸다면… 아마 100%라고 할 수 있겠죠.

1200003695.english: However, even when you were caught secretly working, you said you wouldn't do it again.
1200003695.korean: 하지만 주인님이 몰래 업무를 보시다가 들켰을 때도 다시는 그러지 않겠다는 말을 하셨죠.

1200003696.english: And then, a while ago, he got a used panel... and even on the day we were supposed to spend the night together, he secretly did work...
1200003696.korean: 그래놓고 얼마 전에 중고 패널을 구해서… 같이 밤을 보내기로 한 날에도 몰래 업무를…

1200003698.english: So... you can't be completely reassured even with 100%.

1200003699.english: Beta, I'd like you to show me some evidence so I can trust you 1% more, from 100%.
1200003699.korean: 베타, 100%에서 1%를 더 믿을 수 있도록… 증거를 보여주셨으면 해요.

1200003701.english: Show me proof that you're not attached to the LA Cannoneers.
1200003701.korean: LA 캐노니어즈에 연연하지 않는다는 증거를 보여주세요.

1200003703.english: ...I brought a cheering uniform for the San Francisco Guardians, the Cannoneers' rival team.
1200003703.korean: …캐노니어즈의 라이벌 팀인 샌프란시스코 가디언즈의 응원 유니폼을 가져왔어요.

1200003704.english: If you wear this, I'll trust you and unblock you.
1200003704.korean: 이걸 입는다면 당신을 믿고 차단을 해제하도록 할게요.

1200003706.english: Beta, what are we going to do?

1200003707.english: ...Of course, if I wear that outfit here, I can lift the block.
1200003707.korean: …물론 여기서 저 옷을 입는다면 차단을 풀 수 있겠죠.

1200003708.english: But... in my heart and actions supporting the Cannoneers... there can't be a single speck of falsehood. Supporting the Cannoneers is the very definition of 'justice'.
1200003708.korean: 하지만… 캐노니어즈를 응원하는 저의 마음과 행동에는… 단 한 점의 거짓도 있을 수 없어요. 캐노니어즈를 응원하는 것이야말로 ‘정의’.

1200003710.english: ...I'll be sure to pass on your determination and support to the Arsenal captain, Beta.
1200003710.korean: …당신의 결의와 응원은 꼭 아스널 대장님께 전달하도록 할게요, 베타.

1200003712.english: ...And so, the fierce war among baseball fans came to an end.
1200003712.korean: …그렇게 치열했던 야구팬들 사이의 전쟁은 끝이 나게 되었습니다.

1200003713.english: The results of the war are as follows:

1200003714.english: Elven Forest Maker, Orca Secret Network 30-day ban.
1200003714.korean: 엘븐 포레스트메이커, 오르카 시크릿 네트워크 30일 차단.

1200003715.english: English Shepherd, Orca Secret Network blocked for 7 days.
1200003715.korean: 잉글리쉬 셰퍼드, 오르카 시크릿 네트워크 7일 차단.

1200003716.english: Lemonade Beta, Orca Secret Network blocked for 7 days...!
1200003716.korean: 레모네이드 베타, 오르카 시크릿 네트워크 7일 차단…!

1200003717.english: And so, leaving only wounds for everyone... the First Orca Baseball Fan War came to an end.
1200003717.korean: 그렇게 모두에게 상처만을 남긴 채… 제1차 오르카 야구팬 전쟁이 막을 내렸습니다.

1200003719.english: What's going on now, Mr. Spriggan?
1200003719.korean: 또 무슨 일이 벌어지고 있는 거죠, 스프리건 씨?

1200003722.english: Then, I, Spriggan, will take my leave now...!
1200003722.korean: 그럼, 저 스프리건은 이만 물러가 보겠습니다…!

1200003723.english: Just a moment, Mr. Spriggan! Wait! Don't end it like that!
1200003723.korean: 잠깐만요, 스프리건 씨! 기다리세요! 그렇게 마무리하지 말라고요!

1200003726.english: Then... That's it for this health checkup!

1200003727.english: Let's see... it hasn't changed much from the last checkup results. I think we can say your body has entered a stable phase.
1200003727.korean: 어디 보자… 저번 검진 결과와 크게 달라지지는 않았네. 신체가 안정기에 접어들었다고 봐야 할 것 같아.

1200003728.english: Now I only need to get checked up once or twice a year, right?
1200003728.korean: 이제는 일년에 한두 번 정도만 검진을 받으면 되겠는데?

1200003729.english: ...it's thanks to Dr. [Name] working hard to help. Thank you.

1200003730.english: Oh, come on, Beta, why are you being so polite about something like that?
1200003730.korean: 에이, 베타 언니는 뭘 그런 걸로 예의를 차리고 그래?

1200003731.english: Still, you helped stabilize my body by even cutting back on your sleep time.
1200003731.korean: 그래도 수면 시간까지 줄여가면서 신체 안정화를 도와주셨잖아요.

1200003732.english: ...Well, that's just how it is, we're all family in Orca, so we help each other out.
1200003732.korean: …그거야 뭐, 같은 오르카 식구니까 서로서로 돕고 사는 거지.

1200003733.english: Anyway, I should probably go to bed now. I was up until early yesterday morning answering the questions M333 asked, so I didn't get much sleep and I'm tired...
1200003733.korean: 아무튼, 그럼 난 이만 자러 가 봐야겠다. 어제 새벽까지 엠피트리테 언니가 물어본 것들 답변해 주느라 잠을 설쳤더니 피곤해…

1200003734.english: ...But since they seem like they might join the lab if I just persuade them a little more, I'll have to be patient.
1200003734.korean: …그래도 조금만 더 꼬시면 연구실에 들어올 것 같으니까 내가 참아야지.

1200003736.english: Then have a good night too, sis.

1200003737.english: If you're going to pursue a graduate degree, let me know!

1200003739.english: (Doctor, you're really... mischievous, aren't you? You made that joke there?
1200003739.korean: (닥터 님도 참… 짓궂으시네요. 거기서 그런 농담을 하시다니.)

1200003740.english: (...Ugh, but more importantly... I've been hungry since yesterday because I had a blood test, and I'm a little hungry...)
1200003740.korean: (…으으, 그보다… 피 검사를 하느라 어제부터 굶었더니 조금 배가 고픈데…)

1200003741.english: (At this time, the food court must be closed too... What should I do...?)
1200003741.korean: (지금 시간이면 푸드코트도 다 문을 닫았을 텐데… 어쩌면 좋담...?)

1200003746.english: Do you think it's worth trying?

1200003748.english: ...Okay. Well... shall we try some sales?

1200003751.english: Okay. Is there anyone else here beside you?
1200003751.korean: 그래. 여기 있는 게 당신 밖에 더 있어?

1200003753.english: Anyway, why don't you stay for a cup of tea with us for a bit?
1200003753.korean: 아무튼, 잠깐 우리랑 차나 한 잔 하고 가지 않겠어?

1200003755.english: ...It might be a bit out of the blue, admittedly. But, somehow, I feel like we could have a good conversation.
1200003755.korean: …확실히 조금 뜬금없는 말일지도 모르지. 하지만, 왠지 서로에게 좋은 이야기를 나눌 수 있을 것 같아서 말이야.

1200003759.english: Yes, Ms. Aurora just baked some cookies, or maybe a cake...
1200003759.korean: 네, 아우로라 씨가 방금 구운 과자라던가, 케이크라던가…

1200003761.english: (...Wait a minute, something's not right... It's strange for people I've never met before to suddenly show me this kind of kindness, isn't it...?)
1200003761.korean: (…잠깐만요, 뭐, 뭔가 이상한데요… 처음 보는 분들이 갑자기 이런 호의를 베푸시다니, 이상하잖아요…)

1200003762.english: (I am hungry, but I need to calmly and rationally consider why you would suddenly say something like this...)
1200003762.korean: (배가 고프긴 하지만, 왜 갑자기 이런 말씀을 하시는 것일지 침착하고 냉정하게 생각해 봐야...)

1200003766.english: Having a cup of tea shouldn't be a problem, right?
1200003766.korean: 차 한 잔 하는 것 정도는 문제될 거 없겠죠!

1200003768.english: Okay, here, eat a macaron too.

1200003770.english: You should try the muffins too.

1200003771.english: Is that a blueberry muffin? It's really delicious!

1200003772.english: Of course. Because it's the blueberries Djinnia worked hard to grow.

1200003773.english: You really care deeply about your unit members...

1200003774.english: Alright, you've been waiting a long time! Three special tarts are now available!
1200003774.korean: 자, 오래 기다리셨습니다! 스페셜 타르트 3종 나왔습니다!

1200003776.english: (...Wait, how did this happen...? I was definitely going to refuse, but the word "tart" completely swayed me...!)
1200003776.korean: (…잠깐, 이게 어떻게 된 거죠…? 분명 거절할 생각이었는데… 타르트라는 말에 그만 홀라당 넘어가 버리다니…!)

1200003778.english: (...It's not too late, even now. I should refuse that tart and figure out what Mr. Echidna and Ms. Banshee are thinking...)
1200003778.korean: (…지금이라도 늦지 않았어요. 저 타르트를 거절하고, 에키드나 씨와 밴시 씨가 무슨 생각인지 파악을 해야…)

1200003779.english: Oh, you're not eating? Are you full?

1200003780.english: Oh, no! It was because something suddenly came to mind!
1200003780.korean: 아, 아뇨! 갑자기 뭔가 떠올라서 그런 거였어요!

1200003782.english: Oh, that's a newly developed menu item!

1200003784.english: Yes, it's made with black sapote, a fruit imported from Caracas, instead of chocolate. It tastes like chocolate but is low in calories, so it's very popular in Orca right now.
1200003784.korean: 네, 초콜릿 대신 카라카스에서 수입한 과일인 블랙 사포테를 넣어서 만든 거예요. 초콜릿 맛이 나는데 칼로리가 낮아서, 오르카에서도 지금 인기가 많답니다.

1200003786.english: Um, in that case, would it be alright if I ordered one more?
1200003786.korean: 그, 그럼 혹시 하나 더 주문해도 괜찮을까요?

1200003787.english: Of course! I'll bring it out in a moment!

1200003788.english: So, did you like the tart?

1200003791.english: Heh heh... that's a relief. Actually, what we're about to propose is related to that.
1200003791.korean: 후후… 다행이네. 사실 우리가 제안하려는 것도 그것과 관련이 있거든.

1200003792.english: As you already know well, with the trade with South America this time, various exotic fruits are scheduled to be imported, right?
1200003792.korean: 이미 잘 알고 있겠지만, 이번에 남미와 교역을 하면서 각종 이국적인 과일들이 수입될 예정이잖아?

1200003793.english: Aurora and our gourmet club are very interested in those fruits.
1200003793.korean: 아우로라도 저희 미식회도 그 과일들에 대해서 아주 관심이 많습니다.

1200003794.english: But it's a bit of a burden, considering it's just from the gourmet club's operating expenses.
1200003794.korean: 하지만 아무래도 그건 미식회 운영비만으로는 조금 부담이 되거든.

1200003795.english: So, we'd like to make a suggestion.
1200003795.korean: 그래서 우리가 한 가지 제안을 하고 싶어.

1200003796.english: Actually, the gourmet club has been consistently hiring Aurora, you know?
1200003796.korean: 사실, 미식회에서 아우로라를 꾸준히 고용하고 있거든?

1200003797.english: I'll let you eat the food Aurora makes for free, so I'd like a small discount on the fruit imported from Caracas.
1200003797.korean: 아우로라가 만드는 음식들을 공짜로 먹게 해 줄 테니까, 카라카스에서 수입한 과일을 조금 할인해 줬으면 해.

1200003799.english: I thot you were going to make a terrifying proposal, but it turned out to be nothing that great after all...
1200003799.korean: 엄청 무시무시한 제안을 하시려는 줄 알았는데, 의외로 대단한 건 아니었군요…

1200003800.english: Didn't I say their approach was suspicious?
1200003800.korean: 그러게 접근 방식이 수상하다고 하지 않았습니까.

1200003804.english: Anyway... if I can eat Ms. Aurora's dessert, it's not such a bad offer.
1200003804.korean: 아무튼… 아우로라 씨의 디저트를 먹을 수 있다면 그렇게 나쁜 제안은 아니네요.

1200003805.english: No, rather than that... I'd like to offer you some fruit for free.
1200003805.korean: 아니, 그것보다는 오히려… 공짜로 과일을 제공해 드리고 싶은데요.

1200003809.english: ...Please let me join the gourmet club too.

1200003810.english: So, can I just consider those fruits as if I bought them?
1200003810.korean: 그러면 그 과일들은 제가 사 온 셈 치면 되는 거잖아요?

1200003811.english: ...Well, it's not difficult to get you into the gourmet club, but... you'd go that far?
1200003811.korean: …뭐 미식회에 가입시켜 주는 건 어렵지 않지만… 그렇게까지 해 준다고?

1200003812.english: Well... I have a purpose too.

1200003813.english: ...I'd like to taste Ms. Aurora's food, learn how to make it, and then... I want to make it for the people in Caracas.
1200003813.korean: …아우로라 씨의 음식을 맛보고, 만드는 법을 배운 다음… 카라카스에 계신 분들께 만들어 드리고 싶어서요.

1200003814.english: I owe a lot to the people in Caracas and I'm also very sorry...
1200003814.korean: 카라카스에 계신 분들께 신세진 것도 많고… 죄송한 것도 많아서…

1200003815.english: I really wanted to repay you somehow.
1200003815.korean: 꼭 한번 뭔가 보답을 해 드리고 싶었어요.

1200003816.english: Sweet & Dreams' desserts are famous even among us. If I could offer something like that, I think I could repay the people in Caracas in my own way...
1200003816.korean: Sweet & Dreams의 디저트는 저희들 사이에서도 유명하거든요. 그런 걸 대접할 수 있다면 카라카스에 계신 분들께 나름대로의 보답을 좀 해 드릴 수 있을 것 같아서…

1200003817.english: That's why I'd like to be added to the gourmet club.
1200003817.korean: 그래서 미식회에 가입시켜 주셨으면 하는 거예요.

1200003818.english: That kind of goal... isn't bad.

1200003819.english: I agree. What do you think of Echidna?

1200003820.english: ...Well, if that's the reason, of course I wouldn't refuse.
1200003820.korean: …나도 뭐, 그런 이유라면 거절할 리가 있나.

1200003821.english: ...Congratulations on joining the gourmet club, Beta.

1200003823.english: Then, to celebrate the new member joining the gourmet club... shall we have a party?
1200003823.korean: 그럼 미식회에 새로운 회원이 들어온 기념으로… 파티나 한 번 할까?

1200003824.english: Aurora! Bring some cake over here!

1200003825.english: Huh? I don't have any ingredients prepared separately for the cake...
1200003825.korean: 네? 케이크는 따로 재료를 준비해둔 게 없는데요…?

1200003826.english: Then I can just buy it at the sales office, right?

1200003828.english: Okay, here's a can of tuna.

1200003831.english: As a fee, one additional blanket with the commander's upper body nude.
1200003831.korean: 수고비로 사령관 상반신 누드 이불 하나 추가.

1200003833.english: What flavor of cake do you like?

1200003834.english: I like the taste of coffee!

1200003835.english: The coffee tastes good, doesn't it?

1200003836.english: Yes, I'll make it for you right away!

1200003837.korean: …그나저나, 에키드나는 왜 그렇게 돈이 많은 거죠?

1200003838.english: Oh, it's nothing much... I'm collaborating with Talon Feather to make and sell huggable pillows.
1200003838.korean: 아, 별건 아니고… 탈론페더랑 협업해서 끌어안고 자는 베개를 만들어서 팔고 있거든.

1200003839.english: Good quality sleep is also a pleasure. Then, wouldn't the hugging pillow business, which improves sleep quality, be the domain I, the ruler of all pleasures in this world, should be in charge of?
1200003839.korean: 질 좋은 수면 또한 쾌락. 그렇다면 수면의 질을 높여주는 안는 베개 사업이야말로 이세상 모든 쾌락의 지배자인 내가 담당할 영역 아니겠어?

1200003840.english: Are hugging pillows selling that well?
1200003840.korean: 안는 베개가 그렇게 잘 팔리는 겁니까?

1200003841.english: Well... because they printed the commander's picture on the outside.
1200003841.korean: 그야… 겉에 사령관의 사진을 프린트해 놨거든.

1200003843.english: Heh heh, they say you have to earn well to eat well.
1200003843.korean: 후후, 잘 먹기 위해서는 잘 벌어야 하는 법이라고.

1200003844.english: Anyway, I'll introduce the other members of the gourmet club next time...
1200003844.korean: 아무튼, 다른 미식회 회원들은 다음에 소개해 줄 테니까…

1200003845.english: Nice to meet you, Lemonade Beta?

1200003846.english: I look forward to working with you as well.

1200003848.english: Yes...! Please take care of me, everyone!

1200003850.english: ... Where can I get that hugging pillow?
1200003850.korean: ...그 안는 베개는 어디서 구할 수 있나요?

1200003852.english: The sunshine is warm again today...

1200003854.english: Angel, do we have anything we need to do today?
1200003854.korean: 엔젤, 혹시 오늘 저희가 해야 할 일이 있나요?

1200003855.english: Hmm, let's see... Azazel-sama, Saraqael-sama, and Veronica-sama are out on external support...
1200003855.korean: 글쎄요, 어디 보자… 아자젤님과 사라카엘님, 베로니카님은 외부 지원을 나가셨고…

1200003856.english: Church duties are suspended for the time being.

1200003857.english: I don't think there is anything in particular!

1200003858.english: So... it's a leisurely day off for the first time in a while...

1200003859.english: Hmm... I don't know what to do in a situation like this.
1200003859.korean: 으음… 이럴 때는 어떤 일을 해야 할지 모르겠네요.

1200003860.english: What do other people usually do on holidays?
1200003860.korean: 보통 다른 사람들은 휴일에 무슨 일을 하나요?

1200003861.english: Well, other people... they chat at cafes, pursue their favorite hobbies, or watch movies with their sisters.
1200003861.korean: 글쎄요, 다른 분들은… 카페에서 수다를 떨거나, 좋아하는 취미활동을 하거나, 자매들과 영화를 보거나 하시죠.

1200003864.english: How about we go see a movie for now?
1200003864.korean: 일단은 영화를 보러 오는 건 어때?

1200003865.english: Um... were you talking to us?

1200003866.english: Of course! The werewolf is in a new movie this time, so I'm helping with the promotion.
1200003866.korean: 그럼! 워울프가 이번에 새로운 영화에 출연했거든, 그래서 내가 홍보 역할을 해 주고 있단 말씀.

1200003867.english: Okay, here's the poster. Would you like to dance with the lone outlaw ranger of the wilderness, Wolf? It's a movie called >. It's really fun, so please come and see it at least once!
1200003867.korean: 자, 이게 포스터야. <황야의 고독한 무법자 레인저 울프와 함께 춤을 추지 않으실래요?>라는 영화인데, 진짜 재밌으니까 한 번쯤 보러 와!

1200003869.english: Would you like to dance with the lone outlaw ranger of the wilderness, Wolf?
1200003869.korean: <황야의 고독한 무법자 레인저 울프와 함께 춤을 추지 않으실래요>…?

1200003870.english: The title is a bit confusing...

1200003871.english: Really? I think it would be really fun...!
1200003871.korean: 그런가요? 저는 엄청 재밌을 것 같은데…!

1200003872.english: Well, it's because the titles of several movies are all mixed up...
1200003872.korean: 그게, 여러 영화 제목들이 다 섞여 있어서요…

1200003873.english: So, shall we go see a movie now?

1200003877.english: ...I'm sorry, Angel. But... I don't really like movies...
1200003877.korean: …죄송합니다, 엔젤. 하지만… 저는 영화를 별로 좋아하지 않아서요…

1200003878.english: I'll head in now. Angel... I think it would be better for her to go to the movies with someone else.
1200003878.korean: 이만 들어가 볼게요. 엔젤은… 다른 사람과 함께 영화를 보러 가는 편이 좋을 것 같아요.

1200003880.english: ...But, Lord Ramiel... you actually really like movies, don't you...?
1200003880.korean: …하지만, 라미엘 님은… 사실 영화를 무척 좋아하시잖아요…

1200003881.english: When you were talking about the movie, Ramiel-nim, your expression... it really brightened up. So, the fact that Ramiel-sama likes movies was something I could tell without even reading Ramiel-sama's mind...
1200003881.korean: 영화 이야기를 하실 때 라미엘 님의 표정… 무척 밝아지셨었죠. 그러니, 라미엘 님이 영화를 좋아하신다는 건 라미엘 님의 마음을 읽지 않아도 알 수 있는 사실이었는데…

1200003882.english: Why did you suddenly lie and say you hate movies there...?
1200003882.korean: 대체 왜 갑자기 거기서 영화가 싫다는 거짓말을 하신 걸까…

1200003886.english: ...Then, how can we help Lord Ramiel...?

1200003887.english: ...I guess I'll just have to do that job I was offered before.
1200003887.korean: …역시 전에 제의 받았던 그 일을 하는 수 밖에 없겠군요.

1200003888.english: ...Ramiel-nim, please wait just a little longer. I definitely will...
1200003888.korean: …라미엘 님, 조금만 기다려 주세요. 제가 꼭…

1200003889.english: I'll definitely take you to the movie theater, Ramiel!
1200003889.korean: 제가 꼭 라미엘 님을 영화관에 데려다 드릴 테니까요!

1200003894.english: (What's going on... He came home late yesterday too...)

1200003895.english: (Besides, there was also a faint smell of incense on his body...)

1200003897.english: (The only place that could possibly smell like incense on this Orca is the shrine, and that's it...)
1200003897.korean: (이 오르카에서 향 냄새가 날 만한 곳은 신사, 단 한 곳 뿐인데...)

1200003899.english: I'm disappointed in you, Ramiel, that we can't even go to the movies together!
1200003899.korean: 같이 영화조차 보러 가지 못하는 라미엘 님에게 실망했어요!

1200003900.english: Now I'm going to the shrine to become a priestess, so you, Ramiel, can go to church and pray!
1200003900.korean: 이제 전 무녀가 되기 위해 신사로 갈 테니까, 라미엘 님은 교회에서 예배나 보세요!

1200003901.english: Hoo hoo hoo... very good. I'm transferring to Shinsa...
1200003901.korean: 후후후… 아주 좋네요. 신사로 이적하는 거예요…

1200003902.english: They said I should abandon Ramiel, who won't even watch a movie with me, and just come to the gentleman's club...
1200003902.korean: 같이 영화조차 봐 주지 않는 라미엘은 버리고, 신사로 오면 되는 거랍니다…

1200003903.english: This will further expand the influence of the shrine!

1200003904.english: Alright, Angel! I've prepared a special shrine maiden outfit and underwear just for you!
1200003904.korean: 자, 엔젤! 내 너를 위해 특별히 무녀복과 속옷을 준비해 두었다!

1200003905.english: Come here and try it on quickly...!

1200003906.english: (...Even so, that's too much of a stretch of the imagination.)

1200003907.english: (Harboring such suspicions is questioning Angel's faith in the light. That's why I need to believe in Angel even more during times like these...)
1200003907.korean: (이런 의혹을 품는 것은 빛에 대한 엔젤의 믿음을 의심하는 것. 이럴 때일수록 제가 더욱 엔젤을 믿어줘야 해요…)

1200003908.english: The incense smell coming from Angel's body must have rubbed off from somewhere else. For example...)
1200003908.korean: (엔젤의 몸에서 풍기는 향 냄새는 다른 곳에서 묻어온 게 틀림 없어요. 예를 들면…)

1200003909.english: (The funeral for the business panel you bought used the other day, Savior...)
1200003909.korean: (구원자님께서 저번에 중고로 구매하신 업무용 패널의 장례식이라던가…)

1200003910.english: (...Oh, wait, what's this in Angel's bag...?)

1200003912.english: I hate you, Ramiel, for not even watching movies with me!
1200003912.korean: 같이 영화도 봐 주지 않는 라미엘 님 미워요!

1200003913.english: I'm going to be completely twisted from now on...!

1200003914.english: Here, the strongest five drinks... no, ten drinks... I don't know the name, but the strongest ones here, please!
1200003914.korean: 여기 술… 이름은 모르겠지만, 여기에서 제일 센 술 다섯 잔… 아니 열 잔 주세요!

1200003916.english: Excessive alcohol consumption can lead to stroke, memory loss, or dementia! Please be careful!
1200003916.korean: 지나친 음주는 뇌졸중, 기억력 손상이나 치매를 유발합니다! 조심해 주세요!

1200003917.english: Oh, thank you for the warning.

1200003918.english: Then I'll be going now! Hello!
1200003918.korean: 그럼 저는 이만 가 볼게요! 안녕!

1200003920.english: ...Then give me just one shot of the strongest alcohol you have!
1200003920.korean: …그럼 제일 센 술로 한 잔만 주세요!

1200003923.english: It means there's a terrifyingly large amount of alcohol.

1200003924.english: That, that's a terrifying drink... to that extent!

1200003925.english: ...Okay, I'll drink a lot of this and get drunk!

1200003929.english: This isn't the time to be standing around here, we need to go find Angel quickly...!
1200003929.korean: 여기서 이러고 있을 때가 아니에요, 빨리 엔젤을 찾으러 가야 해요…!

1200003930.english: Don't stray from the path of light, just wait a little longer, Angel!!
1200003930.korean: 빛의 길에서 탈선하지 말고 조금만 기다려주세요, 엔젤!!

1200003933.english: Thanks to Angel-nim's help, I've already finished making this week's batch of mint chocolate meat pies!
1200003933.korean: 엔젤 님이 도와주신 덕분에 이번 주 분량 민트초코 미트파이도 벌써 다 만들었네요!

1200003935.english: ...But, does this mint chocolate meat pie sell that well?
1200003935.korean: …그런데, 이 민트초코 미트파이가 그렇게 잘 팔리나요?

1200003936.english: Of course! Valkyrie and Lilith, and the Master, buy me a lot of things!
1200003936.korean: 물론이죠! 발키리 언니랑, 리리스 언니랑, 주인님이 많이 사 주시거든요!

1200003937.english: But we're almost out of mint chocolate, so starting next week, we'll be selling regular meat pies and cheese meat pies!
1200003937.korean: 그런데 민트초코도 이제 거의 다 떨어져 가서, 다음 주부터는 일반 미트파이랑 치즈 미트파이를 팔 거예요!

1200003939.english: ...I'm busy this week and can't buy any, but I'll try some starting next week!
1200003939.korean: …이번주는 제가 바빠서 못 사먹지만, 다음주부터는 저도 맛보도록 할게요!

1200003941.english: Okay, here's this week's paycheck! I put in extra this week!
1200003941.korean: 그럼, 여기 이번 주 주급이에요! 이번 주는 특별히 많이 넣었어요!

1200003942.english: Please take care of me again next week!

1200003944.english: Okay, with this money... finally, to Lord Ramiel...
1200003944.korean: 좋아, 이 돈이면… 드디어 라미엘 님께…

1200003947.english: Oh, I was just looking for that, so this is perfect. Ramiel-nim, we...
1200003947.korean: 아, 안 그래도 찾고 있었는데 잘 됐네요. 라미엘 님, 저희…

1200003949.english: I'm sorry I didn't go to the movies with you! I was really looking forward to it, but it's leaving theaters tomorrow...
1200003949.korean: 같이 영화를 보러 가지 않아서 미안해요! 엄청 기대하고 있었을 텐데, 내일 극장에서 내려가더라고요…

1200003950.english: So you started disliking me and were saving money to use a different room than me...?
1200003950.korean: 그래서 제가 싫어져서… 저와 다른 방을 쓰려고 돈을 모으고 있던 거죠…?

1200003952.english: That's... sad, but it's okay... But... please don't stray from the path of light...!
1200003952.korean: 그건… 슬프지만 괜찮아요… 하지만… 빛의 길에서 벗어나지는 말아주세요…!

1200003955.english: First, why don't you calm down and talk slowly...?
1200003955.korean: 일단 진정하고 천천히 얘기해 보지 않으실래요…?

1200003958.english: ...I, I wouldn't do that!! I just came home late because I was working a part-time job.
1200003958.korean: …제, 제가 그럴 리가 없잖아요!! 그냥 아르바이트를 하느라 늦게 들어왔던 것 뿐이에요.

1200003959.english: Beside, I know what non-alcoholic means too!

1200003960.english: Then why the business card and the scent...?

1200003961.english: The scent is because she spent time at the shrine while waiting for Hachiko-san...
1200003961.korean: 향은 하치코님을 기다리면서 신사에서 시간을 보내서 그런 거고…

1200003962.english: The business card was given to me when I delivered to Cafe Amor.
1200003962.korean: 명함은 카페 아모르로 배달 갔을 때 받은 거예요.

1200003963.english: Really, it's difficult when you misunderstand like that...

1200003965.english: Then why are you doing this kind of part-time job...?

1200003967.english: It was to rent a movie theater.

1200003969.english: Last time when Ramiel-san received a movie poster from Tomo-san...
1200003969.korean: 저번에 라미엘 님이 토모 님께 영화 포스터를 받았을 때…

1200003970.english: Ramiel-nim's expression was very bright. Even without reading his mind, it's clear that Ramiel-nim loves movies.
1200003970.korean: 라미엘 님의 표정은 무척 밝으셨어요. 마음을 읽지 않아도 라미엘 님이 영화를 좋아한다는 사실을 알 수 있을 정도로요.

1200003971.english: However, Lord Ramiel declined my offer to go see a movie together.
1200003971.korean: 하지만, 라미엘 님은 같이 영화를 보러 가자는 제 제안을 거절하셨죠.

1200003973.english: You're worried your wings might block the screen and bother other viewers, right?
1200003973.korean: 날개가 화면을 가려서, 다른 분들의 관람을 방해할까봐… 맞죠?

1200003975.english: Then if we rent out the movie theater, we won't be bothering anyone else, right?
1200003975.korean: 그럼 영화관을 빌리면 다른 분들께 피해가 가지 않는 거잖아요!

1200003977.english: Thank you, Angel... for being so considerate of me.

1200003978.english: ...Um, but in that case, wouldn't it be enough to rent the back row of the movie theater?
1200003978.korean: …저, 그런데 그런 경우에는 영화관 가장 뒷자리를 빌리면 되지 않나요?

1200003980.english: For those who have trouble watching movies due to tails or wings, Orca Cinema keeps the back row empty!
1200003980.korean: 꼬리나 날개 때문에 영화 감상에 문제가 있는 분들을 위해서, 오르카 영화관에서는 가장 뒷자리를 비워두거든요!

1200003981.english: If Mr. Ramiel borrows the back seat, he'll be able to watch the movie without any problems!
1200003981.korean: 라미엘 씨도 뒷자리를 빌리면 문제없이 영화를 볼 수 있을 거예요!

1200003984.english: Then why am I working part-time...?

1200003986.english: Oh, wouldn't it be great if we bought a bunch of snacks to prepare for when Azazel, Saraqael, and Veronica return?
1200003986.korean: 아, 아자젤과 사라카엘, 베로니카가 돌아올 때를 대비해서 간식을 잔뜩 사 놓으면 되는 거 아니겠어요?

1200003987.english: And we can buy snacks to eat while we watch the movie... right?!
1200003987.korean: 저희가 영화를 보면서 먹을 간식을 사도 되는 거고요…!

1200003988.english: That's right, isn't it? So my efforts weren't in vain, were they?
1200003988.korean: 그, 그렇죠? 제 노력은 쓸모없는 짓이 아니었던 거죠?

1200003989.english: Yes, of course! Thanks to Angel's efforts, we were able to buy a lot of snacks, right?
1200003989.korean: 그, 그럼요! 엔젤이 노력한 덕분에 간식을 잔뜩 사둘 수 있게 된 거잖아요?

1200003990.english: So it wasn't a meaningless action at all!
1200003990.korean: 그러니 결코 의미없는 행동이 아니었던 거예요!

1200003991.english: Okay, let's go buy our snacks then! During that time, you'll be able to buy expensive snacks that you couldn't afford before!
1200003991.korean: 자, 그럼 저희 간식을 사러 가죠! 그 동안 함부로 못 사던 비싼 간식도 마음껏 살 수 있을 거예요!

1200003993.english: - When Angel and Ramiel went to buy snacks, the only one left there was...
1200003993.korean: - 엔젤과 라미엘이 간식을 사러 갔을 때, 그곳에 남아있던 것은 오직…

1200003994.english: - There were only mint chocolate meat pies piled up like mountains.

1200003999.english: - ...and so, without Azazel or Veronica knowing...

1200004000.english: - The tuna can holdings of the Kohei sect have slightly increased.
1200004000.korean: - 코헤이 교단의 참치캔 보유량이 살짝 늘어났다.

1200004001.english: - The Angel Who Works Part-Time! END.

1200004002.english: Orangeade Radio Orca Live! Come on, come on! Start now!  I do!
1200004002.korean: 오렌지에이드 라디오 오르카 라이브! 오라오라! 지금 시작! 합니다!

1200004003.english: Hello, listeners around the world! (Large amount omitted)
1200004003.korean: 안녕하세요, 전 세계의 청취자 여러분! (대량 중략)

1200004004.english: (Large omission) As I announced last time! (Omitted) We have the honor of welcoming Ms. Blind Princess, who was (omitted) in the masterpiece "Dragon Slayer," a collaboration between Bismark and Densetsu!
1200004004.korean: (대량 중략) 지난 번에 예고했던 대로! (중략) 비스마르크와 덴세츠의 합작이었던 희대의 명작 ‘드래곤 슬레이어’의 (중략) 블라인드 프린세스 씨를 이 자리에 모셨습니다!

1200004005.english: Alright! Everyone, applause, applause! Clap! Clap! Clap! Cheers! Wow! It's the same word, but it's amazing! Sister! It's pretty!
1200004005.korean: 자! 모두 박수박수! 짝! 짝! 짝! 환호! 와아아아아아아아아아! 같은 말이지만 환성! 언니! 예뻐요!

1200004011.english: Haha, you must be very tired.

1200004012.english: How long has the broadcast been on?

1200004013.english: Don't worry. It hasn't been long.

1200004015.english: So that's why Merlin felt a sense of rivalry...
1200004015.korean: 멀린이 라이벌 의식을 느끼는 이유가 있었네요…

1200004018.english: Anyway. Blind Princess-nim has come here today to share some good words.
1200004018.korean: 어쨌든. 블라인드 프린세스 씨께서 좋은 말씀 전하려고 이 자리에 오셨는데요.

1200004019.english: That's fresh. Usually, I'm the one who gets called out.
1200004019.korean: 신선하네요. 보통은 제가 태클을 걸리는 쪽인데.

1200004020.english: Mr. Orangeade has a charm that makes me want to tackle him.
1200004020.korean: 오렌지에이드 씨는 제가 태클을 걸고 싶어지게 만드는 매력이 있네요.

1200004022.english: And please don't call me "Mr. Orangeade" stiffly, just call me Orange.
1200004022.korean: 그리고 딱딱하게 오렌지에이드 씨라고 하지 마시고 편하게 오렌지로 불러주세요.

1200004024.english: We've gotten close enough to call each other by nicknames, so please follow me on Social Orca!
1200004024.korean: 줄여서 부를 만큼 친해졌으니 소셜 오르카에서 저를 팔로잉 해주세요!

1200004025.english: I've been advertising my account every broadcast, but I haven't gained as many followers as I expected.
1200004025.korean: 매 방송 때마다 제 계정을 광고하는 데 생각보다 팔로워가 안 늘어서요.

1200004026.english: Then, would you follow me on my panel?
1200004026.korean: 그러면 제 패널로 팔로잉 해 주시겠어요?

1200004027.english: Unlike what it looks like, I can't see in front of me.
1200004027.korean: 보기완 다르게 제가 앞이 안 보이거든요.

1200004028.english: Alpha-nim said we should never joke about disabilities...
1200004028.korean: 장애 관련으로는 절대로 농담하지 말라고 알파 님께서 말씀하셨는데…

1200004029.english: So, the person involved can do it after all!
1200004029.korean: 역시 당사자는 할 수 있는 거군요!

1200004032.english: (They have way more followers than me!)

1200004033.english: (The dark orange within me whispers that if I delete Mr. Blind Princess's account, I'll be one step closer to my goal of being in the top 5 for follower count.)
1200004033.korean: (내 안의 다크 오렌지가 블라인드 프린세스 씨의 계정을 삭제하면 나의 목표 ‘팔로워 수 순위 5위권’에 한 걸음 더 가까워진다고 속삭인다.)

1200004036.english: Even a bad orange is attractive, but a good orange is even more attractive!
1200004036.korean: 나쁜 오렌지도 매력적이지만 착한 오렌지는 더 매력적이라고!

1200004038.english: ...Huh? I'm already following that account, aren't I?

1200004039.english: Is that so? Oh, come to think of it...

1200004040.english: Merlin, come drink with me and listen to the "Ora Ora" song. Then, when Orange said it was impossible for him to lick his elbow, I tried it, and Merlin said, "Bluff, look at yourself!" It's really ugly! I remember following Merlin after he forced him to lick his elbow, saying that.
1200004040.korean: 멀린, 작은 저랑 술을 마시면서 오라오라를 듣다가 오렌지가 자기 팔꿈치를 핥는 건 불가능하다고 말해서 제가 시도하니까 멀린이 ‘블프, 네 모습 봐! 진짜 보기 흉하다!’라고 해서 억지로 멀린이 자기 팔꿈치를 핥게 한 후에 팔로잉 한 기억이 있네요.

1200004042.english: It must have stuck with you because it was still before the third stage.

1200004045.english: Oh, since you said it's impossible to lick your own elbow, something just came to mind.
1200004045.korean: 아, 자기 팔꿈치를 핥는 게 불가능하다고 하니까 생각나는 것이 있는데요.

1200004046.english: I went to Southern Africa on Alpha-nim's mission.
1200004046.korean: 제가 알파 님의 임무로 아프리카 남부에 간 적이 있어요.

1200004048.english: Oh dear, I've been talking about all sorts of things and keeping you, who must be busy, waiting.
1200004048.korean: 아차, 바쁘신 분 모셔놓고 너무 딴 이야기만 했네요.

1200004049.english: I'm not busy. I'm free. The little low-cost airline said, "Staying still is helping," so I diligently stayed still, but...
1200004049.korean: 안 바쁜데요? 한가한데요? 작은 저가 ‘너는 가만히 있는 게 도와주는 거’라고 해서 열심히 가만히 있었는데요?

1200004050.english: I'm so touched by Cyclops Princess-san's considerate words that I can't help but be moved, so the other person won't feel guilty!
1200004050.korean: 상대방이 양심의 가책을 받지 않게 사이클롭스 프린세스 씨의 배려심 넘치는 말에 저도 모르게 감격하게 되네요!

1200004051.english: Getting back to the main point.

1200004052.english: Blind Princess Sea, please share some good words with our listeners.
1200004052.korean: 블라인드 프린세스 씨 오라오라 청취자 여러분께 좋은 말씀 전해주시죠.

1200004053.english: Haha, it's been a while since I've done something like this, so I'm a little nervous.
1200004053.korean: 후후, 오랜만에 이런 일을 하니 조금 긴장이 되네요.

1200004054.english: Can I drink a potion of courage to relax a little?
1200004054.korean: 약간 긴장을 풀기 위한 용기의 물약을 마셔도 될까요?

1200004058.english: It's the elixir of life, after all. I think I'll buy it.

1200004059.english: Then, let's capitalize on this feeling...

1200004061.english: Hello? Are you enjoying the broadcast?

1200004062.english: Most people who listen to Ora Ora are probably doing their best to survive.
1200004062.korean: 오라오라를 듣는 분들 대부분은 살아남기 위해서 최선을 다하고 계시겠죠.

1200004063.english: Telling people who are already doing their best to survive to keep going is going to sound harsh.
1200004063.korean: 이미 살아남기 위해서 최선을 다 하시는 분들께 더 힘내라는 말은 가혹하게 들릴 거예요.

1200004064.english: So I won't tell you to work harder than you are now.
1200004064.korean: 그래서 지금보다 더 힘내라는 말은 하지 않겠어요.

1200004065.english: You who are still alive after humanity's extinction!

1200004068.english: Thanks to your presence, we were able to find strength.
1200004068.korean: 여러분께서 계신 덕분에 저희들은 힘을 낼 수 있었어요.

1200004069.english: If we were the only ones on this harsh planet, we wouldn't have been able to muster this kind of strength.
1200004069.korean: 이 험난한 행성에 저희들만 있었다면 이렇게 저희들도 힘을 낼 수 없었을 거예요.

1200004070.english: As you know, I was born to be an actor.

1200004071.english: Actors are necessary for making movies. Movies need people to watch them.
1200004071.korean: 배우는 영화를 찍기 위해 필요하고. 영화는 봐주는 사람이 필요하죠.

1200004072.english: Similarly, for us to fight, there need to be people who need our help.
1200004072.korean: 이것과 마찬가지로 저희가 싸우기 위해서는 저희들의 도움이 필요한 사람들이 있어야 해요.

1200004073.english: Even when I'm so exhausted I could collapse, thinking of those who need our help gives me the strength to keep going.
1200004073.korean: 힘들어서 쓰러질 지경이 되어도 우리들의 도움을 필요로 하는 사람들을 생각하면 다시 힘을 낼 수 있죠.

1200004074.english: Even if you can't see it, you can know.

1200004075.english: Life is strong, after all. That you are out there, living your lives diligently somewhere in the world.
1200004075.korean: 생명은 강하니까. 세상 어딘가에서 열심히 살아있는 여러분이 있다는걸요.

1200004076.english: Saying you'll save everyone is arrogant.

1200004078.english: But Orca will work hard to save you, fueled by this arrogance.
1200004078.korean: 하지만 오르카는 이 오만함을 동력으로 열심히 여러분을 구하려 할 거예요.

1200004081.english: Just knowing you're alive gives us a lot of strength!
1200004081.korean: 여러분께서 살아주시는 것만으로도 저희들에게는 큰 힘이 되니까요!

1200004082.english: And if you ever need our Orca's strength, please let us know!
1200004082.korean: 그리고 우리 오르카의 힘이 필요하면 언제든지 말해주세요!

1200004083.english: Just as I received help from Orca, Orca, including myself, will help you.
1200004083.korean: 제가 오르카에 도움을 받았던 것처럼 저를 포함한 오르카는 여러분을 돕겠어요.

1200004084.english: Anywhere on Earth. No! If you need Orca's help anywhere in the universe, Orca will come running!
1200004084.korean: 지구 어디에서든. 아니! 우주 어디에서든 오르카의 도움이 필요하다면 오르카가 달려갈게요!

1200004085.english: And then, when we meet, it's "Hello." They greet each other by saying, "Hello."

1200004087.english: That's all I wanted to say.

1200004088.english: It's a good night here, but I'll say it for everyone in the world.
1200004088.korean: 여기에선 좋은 밤이지만 세상의 모든 분들을 위해 말할게요.

1200004089.english: Good morning, good afternoon, good evening, good night, and...
1200004089.korean: 좋은 아침, 좋은 오후, 좋은 저녁, 좋은 밤 그리고……

1200004090.english: What do you say to those in space?

1200004091.english: Um... should I go ask the Orbital Watchers?
1200004091.korean: 어… 오비탈 와쳐 분들께 여쭤보고 올까요?

1200004095.english: Heh heh, for a speech I just made up on the spot, was it any good?

1200004096.english: I forgot and left the script my little brother made for me.
1200004096.korean: 깜빡하고 작은 저가 만들어준 대본을 두고 왔거든요.

1200004098.english: Of course I can't read it. Because I can't see. So I don't even know what he wrote.
1200004098.korean: 당연히 못 읽죠. 눈이 안 보이니까. 그래서 뭐라고 써줬는지도 몰라요.

1200004099.english: Um... is there a recording of the broadcast script file on this panel?
1200004099.korean: 어… 여기 패널에 방송용 대본 녹음본 파일이 있네요?

1200004100.english: As expected, it's me, the small one! You're well-prepared.

1200004102.english: Even a small me would feel the same way.
1200004102.korean: 작은 저도 저랑 같은 마음일 거예요.
